mnemonics.title: F-Distribution Mean Formula
mnemonics.content: Remember "D2 over D2 minus 2" -- the mean E(F) = d2/(d2-2) depends ONLY on the Denominator df. Think: "the Floor (F) is held up by the Denominator." Valid only when d2 > 2.

mnemonics.title: Numerator on Top, Denominator on Bottom
mnemonics.content: To remember which df is which: "d1 is the Numerator (N comes before D alphabetically, 1 comes before 2). The LARGER variance always goes on TOP (numerator)." Mnemonic: "First on top, Second on the bottom" (F = s1^2/s2^2 with s1^2 > s2^2).

mnemonics.title: T-Squared Gives F(1,n)
mnemonics.content: Remember the relationship T^2 = F(1, n) with the phrase: "T-Squared is the First F" -- the numerator df is always 1 (the 'first' and smallest possible). So if T ~ t(n), then T^2 ~ F(1, n). The '1' in F(1, n) matches the single T being squared.
